summary: Define M3 historical claim pipeline for names, titles, and relationships
acceptance_criteria: A documented M3 claim pipeline exists for names, titles, kinship, office claims, polity relationships, and vassalage assertions, using existing historical-claim schema fields and the source-policy rules in docs/10-historical-research-policy.md.

The pipeline distinguishes source statements, scholarly interpretation, project inference, and game abstraction, and it records confidence and HISTORICAL/INFERRED/COMPOSITE/FICTIONAL status for any specific claim.
The pipeline explicitly blocks formal historical people, titles, relationships, symbols, or polity claims from entering content-source or runtime fixtures without source IDs and claim records.
Any examples are abstract validation examples only and cannot be mistaken for formal historical content.
No production code, package code, content-source data, gameplay implementation, roadmap edit, accepted ADR, or gate file is modified.
required_tests: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M3-HISTORICAL-CLAIM-PIPELINE-001 and any changed schema/template JSON files
Markdown/schema lint or targeted parse command for the new claim-pipeline artifact
notes: This is a pipeline and policy task only; it must not create formal historical character, title, relationship, or polity content.
If the task needs to change the historical claim schema in a way that alters accepted policy semantics, route to systems_architect before continuing.
M3 character fixtures may depend on this pipeline while remaining synthetic or explicitly claim-labeled.
